spots.name: Eiffel Tower
spots.description: The iconic iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars — best viewed at sunset.

spots.name: Sacré-Cœur
spots.description: The white basilica atop Montmartre with sweeping views of Paris.

spots.name: Musée d'Orsay
spots.description: Impressionist masterpieces housed in a stunning Beaux-Arts railway station.

spots.name: Le Marais
spots.description: Historic neighborhood with art galleries, vintage shops, and hidden courtyards.

spots.name: Café de Flore
spots.description: Iconic Parisian café in Saint-Germain, once home to Sartre and de Beauvoir.

spots.name: Coutume Café
spots.description: Third-wave specialty coffee in a beautifully minimal space.

spots.name: Boot Café
spots.description: Tiny, charming café tucked away in the Marais — excellent flat whites.

spots.name: Le Bouillon Chartier
spots.description: Belle Époque dining hall serving classic French dishes at unbeatable prices.

spots.name: Chez Janou
spots.description: Provençal bistro famous for its legendary chocolate mousse.

spots.name: Pink Mamma
spots.description: Four-story Italian restaurant with rooftop terrace and incredible pasta.

spots.name: Le Syndicat
spots.description: Innovative cocktail bar focusing exclusively on French spirits.

spots.name: Candelaria
spots.description: Hidden speakeasy behind a taquería — some of the best cocktails in Paris.

spots.name: Le Perchoir Marais
spots.description: Rooftop bar with panoramic views and creative cocktails.

spots.name: Rue Crémieux
spots.description: A colorful, pastel-painted pedestrian street — Paris's most photogenic block.

spots.name: Petite Ceinture
spots.description: Abandoned railway turned urban nature trail running through hidden parts of the city.

spots.name: Palais Royal Gardens
spots.description: Serene arcaded gardens with Buren's striped columns — a secret oasis.
